title: How to Measure the Unmeasurable
excerpt: You can't measure trust, satisfaction, or quality directly. But you can measure things that reliably track them. Proxy variables are the most creative tool in data science — and the most misused.

title: When the Data Reveals the Unexpected
excerpt: Rain makes your reviews worse. Hurricanes spike Pop-Tart sales sevenfold. Sunshine moves stock markets. What these discoveries share — and what they teach us about data.

title: What Does a Data Scientist Actually Do in a Political Campaign?
excerpt: Not poll aggregation. Not election forecasting. The real work is messier, more creative, and far more interesting than the public narrative suggests.
